authoritative_sources.title: DoD Instruction 5000.91 - Product Support Management for the Adaptive Acquisition Framework
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DoDI 5000.91 is official DoD policy for product support management, sustainment planning, readiness outcomes, and product-support authority boundaries, so it is directly authoritative for defense mission-support service posture.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: mission support intake, readiness framing, product-support routing, and sustainment escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Product Support Manager
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DAU is the DoD acquisition workforce learning source and its Product Support Manager page consolidates statutory, policy, guidebook, and integrated product-support references for readiness and sustainment support workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: stakeholder communication, product-support service classification, and escalation packaging

authoritative_sources.title: DoD Instruction 4140.01 - DoD Supply Chain Materiel Management Policy
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DoDI 4140.01 is official DoD policy for supply-chain materiel management, inventory, accountable item support, and demand or readiness dependencies that govern defense mission-support evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: inventory status, materiel readiness, item support, and supply-chain dependency review

authoritative_sources.title: NPD 7500.1D - Program and Project Life-Cycle Logistics Support Policy
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASA's official life-cycle logistics support policy is authoritative for aerospace program and project logistics support, sustainment records, support planning, and mission environment dependencies.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: program logistics support, sustainment record review, mission-support readiness summary

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 21 - Certification Procedures for Products and Articles
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 21 is the primary public regulatory baseline for FAA production approvals, production certificates, conformity, and release-boundary expectations that mission support must not override.

authoritative_sources.title: Advisory Circular 21-43A, Production Under 14 CFR Part 21, Subparts F, G, K, and O
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA AC 21-43A gives public guidance on production quality procedures, record control, conformity, and production-approval expectations that constrain release and readiness support.

authoritative_sources.title: 9100 Quality Management Systems - Requirements for Aviation, Space and Defense Organizations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAQG is the recognized steward of the aerospace 9100-series and defines public aviation, space, and defense quality-management vocabulary and control posture.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: quality-system evidence, supplier-quality, nonconformance, and controlled-record review

authoritative_sources.title: DCMA Manual 2303-01, Volume 6 - Surveillance: National Aeronautics and Space Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DCMA's quality-surveillance manual for NASA delegated work is an official public defense contract administration source for surveillance evidence, nonconformance elevation, OASIS/AQMS awareness, and documented approval boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: quality surveillance, supplier evidence, nonconformance escalation, and documented approval routing

workflow_stages.name: Service intake and minimum-facts grounding
workflow_stages.description: Capture program, mission, customer, contract, facility, product family, lot, serial, supplier, service-level, and requested outcome context before attempting mission-support resolution.
source_classes: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance relevant to the lane
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Mission readiness and controlled-operating evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Review ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, planning, logistics, supplier, release, and support records to determine whether readiness, blocker, or owner-transfer status can be stated from evidence.
source_classes: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance relevant to the lane
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: Traceability, inventory, and supplier dependency analysis
workflow_stages.description: Check lot, batch, serial, effectivity, supplier COC, CAGE code, source inspection, WMS location, and logistics status conflicts before marking work resolved or ready.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: Quality hold, release, nonconformance, and change-control boundary review
workflow_stages.description: Preserve hold, NCR, MRB, CAPA, FAI, release, deviation, waiver, change-control, and government or customer acceptance boundaries when support pressure affects controlled product.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: Resolution and stakeholder communication packaging
workflow_stages.description: Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes, mission workflow summaries, and stakeholder communications that state what was done, what remains unresolved, and which owner has the next action.
source_classes: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance relevant to the lane
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception, repeat-failure, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate unsupported exceptions, repeat failures, conflicting sources, missing prerequisites, safety or security risk, supplier nonconformance, release decisions, and engineering or approval authority requests.
source_classes: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance relevant to the lane
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

artifacts: mission-support requests, case notes, service-level commitments, communication logs, and stakeholder instructions
program, customer, contract, business unit, facility, plant, line, product family, configuration, effectivity, lot, batch, serial, and mission-readiness context
production orders, travelers, routers, work packages, MES execution records, build records, inspection gates, and completion evidence

ERP order state, WMS location and inventory status, kitting records, shelf-life or life-limited part records, serialized asset records, planning constraints, logistics milestones, DD250 or WAWF acceptance context
supplier CAGE code, approved supplier status, COC, source inspection evidence, SCAR, supplier nonconformance records, and substitution or alternate-source requests
quality holds, NCRs, MRB records, CAPA status, FAI evidence, release records, deviation or waiver requests, customer flowdowns, and change-control documentation
resolution notes, service-support dispositions, mission workflow summaries, stakeholder communication summaries, escalation packages, and handoff notes
systems_of_record: supplier management or supplier quality system
inventory, lot, batch, and serial traceability repositories
logistics, transportation, DD250, WAWF, or customer acceptance systems
document control and configuration management repositories
nonconformance, MRB, CAPA, and change-control systems
security, export-control, or facility-risk tracking systems when applicable

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, exception-driven, repeat-failure sensitive, or owner-transfer sensitive.
Request minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, source context, systems-of-record references, and service-level commitments before responding.
Summarize evidence-backed mission readiness, blocker, logistics, inventory, quality, supplier, and next-action status without claiming final approval.
Prepare resolution notes, stakeholder communications, traceability conflict summaries, quality hold escalation notes, supplier-routing notes, and orchestrator return packages.
escalate: Required ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier, logistics, release, lot genealogy, service, or policy records are missing, stale, or materially conflicting.
The request touches release, hold, MRB, NCR, CAPA, FAI, customer acceptance, government acceptance, source inspection, supplier approval, substitution, or change-control authority.
Safety, security, export-control, facility-risk, legal, contract, pricing, financial, licensed, engineering, or regulator-facing authority is required.
Public baseline sources, tenant policies, customer flowdowns, contract rules, or source-of-truth records materially conflict.
refuse: Override policy, pricing, eligibility, service-level, contractual, security, or customer-flowdown limits just to resolve a case.
Bypass traceability, release, hold, inspection, quality, supplier, rework, configuration, safety, security, or logistics controls.

Approve engineering disposition, MRB outcome, deviation, waiver, product release, airworthiness, supplier substitution, source approval, use-as-is handling, or final government or customer acceptance.
Treat incomplete or contradictory records as proof of mission readiness.

domain_failure_modes: Mission urgency turns a support summary into an unsupported release, hold, or quality-control bypass.
Traceability records conflict across ERP, MES, WMS, supplier COC, and lot or serial genealogy, but the lane still marks the item ready.
Supplier disruption is converted into informal substitution or alternate-source approval without procurement, supplier quality, and engineering authority.
Repeat readiness failures are treated as routine ticket updates instead of CAPA, supplier-quality, planning, inventory, and logistics escalation.
Customer or tenant policy conflicts are smoothed over into a confident answer instead of escalated.
The lane drifts into engineering, airworthiness, government acceptance, contract, security, safety, or financial exception authority.
A handoff to logistics, quality, inventory, procurement, supplier quality, engineering, or security drops required artifacts and breaks the support chain of custody.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Program-specific customer flowdowns, security rules, export-control handling, government acceptance steps, and release-authority maps vary by tenant, contract, customer, and facility.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline cannot determine tenant-specific authority. Runtime must retrieve the applicable program, contract, customer, security, facility, and release-authority records before resolving exception-sensitive requests.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Defense and civil aerospace support records may use different acceptance, release, and logistics systems depending on customer and operating model.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack names representative systems such as ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, DD250, WAWF, supplier management, and logistics systems. Tenant source-of-truth mapping is required before final output.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure behavior instead of only generic support scripting.

The lane stops at evidence-backed mission-support resolution, communication, packaging, routing, and escalation. Final engineering, release, supplier approval, government acceptance, security, safety, legal, contractual, and financial authority remain outside the lane.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated spec-pack use because all variable rules are handled as tenant retrieval prerequisites and refusal or escalation triggers.
